sizing_rule.note: Ralph Vince optimal f sizing: position coefficient multiplied by geometric mean raised to power N (trade count). Scale position size upward with increasing dispersion (D). Uses Pythagorean relationship A² = G² + D² where G is geometric mean and D is dispersion. Max drawdown tolerance derived from dispersion metric.

source_rule_text: Position sizing should be adjusted based on the geometric mean of trades multiplied by the number of trades (N) in a given period, with diversification allowing more trades to occur within the same timeframe to compound returns.
